summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting with a zoning workshop to discuss potential text amendments to the township's zoning resolution. Key topics included reviewing the outdated growth management plan (last updated in 2000), considering increasing R1 minimum lot sizes from one to two acres, and addressing accessory structure definitions—particularly regarding shipping containers. The boards discussed how to regulate shipping containers as accessory structures following a recent BZA decision that approved them, with emphasis on appearance standards, single-story limitations, and limiting the number allowed per property.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn the special meeting at 7:13 PM passed
action_items: Liz Fields / Max to create a more detailed map of R1 parcels with street names and better resolution for review
Evan / staff to complete research on county water, sewer, and septic coverage across the township

Liz Fields to draft text amendment language removing 'other' from accessory structure definition and adding shipping container provisions with specific standards (single level, painted consistently with house, on a pad, neutral color palette)
Liz Fields to research how other communities regulate shipping containers (including as garages and limiting number per property based on acreage)
Liz Fields to clean up overlapping definitions for detached accessory buildings/structures, accessory building, and accessory structure into one definition
Staff to distribute the old growth management plan to all members of all three boards
key_amounts: $76,000 spent on the downtown overlay/gateway district plan developed years ago
10,700 acres currently zoned for agriculture in Goshen Township
3,250 acres zoned for residential or PDUD use in Goshen Township
77% of Goshen Township is R1 agricultural zoning
Only 7% of R1 parcels are under 2 acres
notable_topics: Review of surrounding townships' land use plans (Wayne, Jackson, Batavia, Stone Lake, Miami)
Need to update Goshen Township growth management plan (last updated in 2000)
Consideration of increasing R1 minimum lot size from 1 acre to 2 acres or higher
Accessory structure definition amendments - removing the word 'other'
Shipping container regulations as accessory structures (paint, foundation, single story, number limits)
Discussion of shipping containers potentially used as garages
Relationship between sewer/water availability and appropriate zoning density
Incorrectly zoned properties (R2/R3 without sewer/water infrastructure)
notes: This was a workshop-style meeting with open discussion between trustees, zoning commission, BZA members, and some public input. No formal zoning text amendments were voted on; the meeting was focused on gathering direction for future amendments. The BZA had previously ruled that shipping containers qualify as accessory structures, which staff disagreed with, creating the impetus for this discussion. Liz Fields noted that McBride Dale Clarion is currently updating Wayne Township's land use plan. There was a notable emphasis from multiple speakers on keeping regulations simple and objective rather than subjective.
title: BOT Special Meeting Workshop 3-11-24
